method.population_source: U.S. Census Bureau Vintage 2025 incorporated-place estimates
method.selection: Next sixteen largest incorporated U.S. cities above 100,000 population not already represented by BID Atlas or included in the first five large-city batches
method.active_hit_definition: At least one currently operating assessment-funded BID or locally named legal equivalent supported by an official source
method.notes: Candidate discovery and production boundary ingestion are performed together where an active district is verified. TIF-only, CRA-only, entertainment, residential, and capital-infrastructure districts are excluded. Cities already represented by a mapped district were skipped. Pasadena, California (Census rank 216) was skipped because its active districts are already represented.

records.local_term: Proposed downtown business improvement district (not formed)
records.summary: No operating assessment-funded BID or tourism district was verified in Orange. City planning records proposed a self-funded downtown BID in the late 1990s, but current downtown cleaning, parking, economic-development, and event programs are procured or funded directly by the City and no formation resolution, management district plan, assessment levy, or active district operator was identified.
records.production_next_action: Monitor for a current downtown or lodging assessment formation proceeding; do not map the historic district, downtown planning area, or City-funded service area.

records.summary: Cedar Rapids has active Downtown and Medical Self-Supported Municipal Improvement Districts. The property-tax-supported Downtown SSMID funds enhanced downtown management and programming through the Cedar Rapids Metro Economic Alliance. The MedQuarter SSMID covers roughly 55 blocks and supports marketing, beautification, business development, district services, and implementation of its current master plan through a mayor- and council-appointed commission.
records.production_next_action: Production-ingested as two generalized downtown benefit areas; replace with current parcel-derived SSMID boundaries when available.

records.local_term: Redevelopment and special assessment areas (place-management district not verified)
records.summary: No operating assessment-funded commercial place-management or tourism district was verified in West Valley City. Current economic-development work is delivered through the City and redevelopment agency, while special assessment tools are used for infrastructure rather than supplemental cleaning, safety, marketing, events, or business support.
records.production_next_action: Monitor for a commercial-area management assessment or lodging marketing assessment; do not map redevelopment, taxing, or infrastructure assessment areas.

records.summary: Hampton has two active commercial-real-estate-tax-supported BIDs: the Coliseum Central Business Improvement District and Downtown Hampton Business Improvement District. The City's FY2025 financial report confirms that both levy additional taxes to fund district promotion, development, and marketing through their respective nonprofit management organizations.
records.production_next_action: Production-ingested as two generalized commercial benefit areas; replace with current tax-parcel boundaries when available.

records.local_term: Downtown Development Authority / proposed BID tool (excluded)
records.summary: No operating special-assessment-funded BID or principal shopping district was verified in Warren. The active Downtown Development Authority uses a tax-increment-finance plan, and the Van Dyke corridor study recommends a BID as a possible future management framework rather than documenting formation or an assessment levy.
records.production_next_action: Monitor the Van Dyke corridor recommendation for a formation ordinance and first special assessment; do not map the DDA/TIF district.
